headline: Grocery Prices Up 22% in 3 Years While Wages Grow 6%
context: DOSM's Consumer Price Index data showed food-at-home prices rose 22% cumulatively from 2023 to 2025, far outpacing nominal wage growth of 6% over the same period, with vegetables, fish, and eggs showing the steepest increases.

cards.big: Eggs up 34%, kangkung up 28%, ikan kembung up 41% — grocery bills devour ever more of Malaysian incomes
cards.sub: Average family of 4 now spends RM1,840 monthly on food versus RM1,510 in 2022 — a RM330 monthly increase

cards.big: Food inflation at 22% versus wage growth of 6% means real food affordability declined 16% in 3 years
cards.sub: Malaysia imports 60% of food by value — weak ringgit amplifies global food price increases domestically

cards.big: B40 families report cutting meals from 3 to 2 per day — 18% of surveyed households in UNICEF 2025 study
cards.sub: Child stunting rate in Kelantan and Sabah at 28% — among highest in Southeast Asia

cards.big: KPDN price controls cover only 12 essential items — 280 common grocery products have zero price regulation
cards.sub: Price ceiling enforcement relies on 890 officers monitoring 142,000 retail premises — 1 officer per 160 shops

cards.big: When families choose between meals, the cost of living crisis isn't a statistic — it's a child going to school hungry
Should Malaysia establish strategic food reserves and government-run wholesale markets to break middleman price inflation?
